title: Apple galette
subtitle: Free-form shortcrust pastry tart with spiced apples
excerpt: A rough, free-form pastry tart filled with spiced apples, folded at the edges rather than fitted into a tin. The imprecision is the point: the pastry can be any shape, the filling any depth. Serves six.
sourceNotes: The free-form tart (galette) is documented in French domestic cookery from the nineteenth century as a rustic pastry preparation made without a tin. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: a free-form pastry preparation with fruit filling and folded edges is documented in the pastry chapter as a domestic alternative to the formal tart. The apple filling is consistent with the British and French autumn fruit tart tradition documented throughout the nineteenth century.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best served warm on the day. Reheat in a low oven for 10 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the pastry should be golden and crisp and the apples soft with caramelised edges; 35–40 minutes

content.text: The pastry for a galette should be slightly short and crumbly rather than smooth and elastic; an imperfect, cratered crust is correct. Roll it on lightly floured paper so it can be transferred to the tray without handling. Leave a 6 cm border around the apples before folding; any narrower and the fold will not hold.

items.prepNote: about 4 medium, peeled, cored, and sliced 5 mm thick
for scattering over the pastry edge

content.text: Combine the {{plain-flour}}, cold {{unsalted-butter}}, {{caster-sugar}} (pastry portion), and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a bowl. Rub the butter in until the mixture looks like rough, uneven crumbs with some pea-sized pieces remaining. Add 3 to 4 tablespoons of cold water and press together until the dough just coheres; it should feel rough rather than smooth. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Toss the {{apple-eating}} slices with the {{caster-sugar}} (filling portion), {{cinnamon-ground}}, and {{lemon}} zest and juice. Leave for 10 minutes while the oven preheats to 200°C fan.

Roll the chilled pastry on a lightly floured sheet of baking paper to a rough circle of about 32 cm; it should be about 4 mm thick and irregular at the edges. Slide the paper onto a flat baking tray. Arrange the {{apple-eating}} slices in overlapping concentric circles in the centre, leaving a 6 cm border all round. Fold the border up and over the outer edge of the apples, pressing the folds down lightly to hold them. Brush the folded pastry edge with the beaten {{eggs}} glaze and scatter the {{demerara-sugar}} over the pastry. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the pastry is deep gold and the apples are soft with caramelised edges. Rest on the tray for 10 minutes before serving.
